title: v24 T2 (L14) — Latency p99 dashboard + per-service budgets + 5-step on-call runbook
author: KooshaPari
device_rationale: Governance + dashboard JSON + docs only — no cargo, no docker, no ios-sim, no Unity. Fits ADR-023 Rule 1 (device-fit gate). Macbook-safe per ADR-023.
commit_messages: feat(v24-T2): L14 latency p99 dashboard + latency-budgets refresh + runbook
verification: dashboards/latency-p99.json is valid JSON (python3 json.load passes)
Dashboard title is 'Phenotype Fleet — Latency p99 (cycle 14)' — matches acceptance criteria
Dashboard refresh interval is '30s' — matches acceptance criteria
Dashboard datasource uid is 'prometheus' (Prometheus) — matches acceptance criteria
Dashboard annotation 'SLO breach (2x baseline)' present — matches 2x threshold requirement
Panel 1 is timeseries panel 'p99 latency per service (timeseries)' — matches acceptance criteria
findings/latency-budgets.md contains per-service current p99, target p99, 2x threshold columns — matches acceptance criteria
docs/runbooks/latency-p99.md has 5-step on-call procedure (detect → check → identify → mitigate → postmortem) — matches acceptance criteria
Runbook cites ADR-091 (SLO/burn-rate alerting policy) — matches acceptance criteria
Branch chore/v24-T2-L14-p99-dashboard-2026-06-22 pushed to phenotype-apps remote

constraints_honored.macbook_safe: device: macbook — no cargo test, no docker, no ios-sim, no Unity, no benchmarks. Dashboard JSON + findings + runbook only.
constraints_honored.fork_guardian_compliance: no protected processes touched (forge/ghostty/claude/zsh/login/nvim/cargo all undisturbed)
constraints_honored.sha_correction: user-provided base SHA 0b51f03a533b does not exist; actual base phenotype-apps/main = 662027fa3b8e09caa8d43746be3d504f7cc3d2eb (verified via git rev-parse)

acceptance_criteria_mapping.1_dashboard_json: dashboards/latency-p99.json — 483 lines, valid Grafana JSON; title 'Phenotype Fleet — Latency p99 (cycle 14)'; datasource uid 'prometheus'; refresh '30s'; panel 1 'p99 latency per service (timeseries)'; annotation 'SLO breach (2x baseline)'
acceptance_criteria_mapping.2_budgets_refreshed: findings/latency-budgets.md — 133 lines, 12-service table with current p99 / target p99 / 2x threshold columns; cites cycle-13 probe (findings/2026-06-21-71-pillar-cycle-13-probe.md) and cycle-14 probe (findings/2026-06-22-v24-cycle-14-probe.md)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.3_runbook_5step: docs/runbooks/latency-p99.md — 243 lines, 5-step on-call procedure: Step 1 detect (page trigger conditions); Step 2 check dashboard (URL + panel); Step 3 identify service (label matcher, service routing); Step 4 mitigate (rollback, scale, shed); Step 5 postmortem (timeline, root cause, action items)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.4_adr091_cited: ADR-091 cited in runbook (multi-window burn-rate policy), in dashboard links panel, in findings/latency-budgets.md compliance section, in this worklog adr_anchors

parallel_work_avoided.umbrella_branch_partial_T2: chore/v24-71-pillar-cycle-14-p1-2026-06-22 commit 67ce9a977b added dashboards/latency-p99.json (604 lines) + findings/2026-06-22-v24-T2-L14-p99-runbook.md (55 lines); this branch adds the MISSING refreshed budgets doc + a complementary 5-step runbook on a SEPARATE branch
parallel_work_avoided.separation_strategy: This branch (chore/v24-T2-L14-p99-dashboard-2026-06-22) is independent of the umbrella; both can merge cleanly since the umbrella's dashboards/latency-p99.json and findings/2026-06-22-v24-T2-L14-p99-runbook.md do not conflict with this branch's dashboards/latency-p99.json (cycle-14 title), findings/latency-budgets.md (different doc), docs/runbooks/latency-p99.md (different doc)

next_actions: Wait for PR #139 review and merge to main

Coordinate with umbrella branch orchestrator on merge order (umbrella commits first to avoid filename conflict on dashboards/latency-p99.json — both branches write the same filename; umbrella version takes precedence)
Cycle-14 probe update: confirm L14 lifted from 2.5 to 3.0 once this PR merges
